=== THE LAW ===
ATTRIBUTED sentence -> ENTAILED by its source's verbatim span (direction, number+unit, modality, scope).
OWNED -> reviewer's voice, no source (oblique or direct), no new particular, no bare empirical finding.
An ACCEPTED MANUSCRIPT is never the journal version. Fail closed on uncertainty -- BUT a validator that
rejects TRUE findings when its judge is unreachable is output-dead, not safe.

=== STATE (verified by me, the orchestrator, running the tests myself) ===
* 8/8 fabrication burns are behaviourally CLOSED (synonym/magnitude/scope/modality/table/venue/verdict/
  oblique-source). The real entailment judge is on the path and fails closed. Independently confirmed.
* BUT the canary is RED on 2 FALSE POSITIVES: 'a TRUE finding reaches the page' and 'CROSS-SOURCE SYNTHESIS
  survives'. Cause: the judge fails closed, and in the test env there is no judge stub, so it rejects
  LEGITIMATE entailed findings. This is the turn-1 failure (163 real sentences deleted) resurfacing.
* P0 hops 5 & 7 are OPEN (an accepted manuscript can be ruled the journal at two hops before the final
  citation gate).


YOUR TASK: close P0 hops 5 & 7. An accepted manuscript is ruled the journal at two hops before the final
citation gate. Both were found by a fresh adversary, both pinned:

HOP 7 (severest) -- /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/alignment_census.py:111 is_publisher_typeset -- THE COUNTERFEIT TYPESET.
  It rules a doc publisher-typeset on >=3 page-folios inside the declared page range, but NEVER SEGMENTS
  OFF THE REPOSITORY COVER SHEET. A Nature Communications accepted manuscript whose COVER SHEET cites
  '14(1): 1-12' and whose body paginates from 1 is ruled the JOURNAL ARTICLE (folios 1-10 land in [1,12]).
  declared_page_range (:54), folio_numbers (:49), page_top_heads (:73) all scan the WHOLE text including
  the cover sheet. FIX: segment the cover sheet off FIRST (provenance.segment_cover_sheet /
  event_ledger.segment_front_matter already exist -- reuse one), then derive typeset facts from the
  article body only. On the SAME bytes, provenance.derive_expression_kind correctly returns
  accepted_manuscript -- the census must AGREE with it, not overrule it.

HOP 5 -- /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/event_ledger.py:411 _ACCEPTED_STAMP -- DETECTOR DRIFT. Hop 6 widened
  provenance._AM_MARK (:441) to catch the NIH tell ('author manuscript;? *available in pmc',
  '\bnihms\s*\d{3,}'); that clause was NEVER added to event_ledger's _ACCEPTED_STAMP. The two
  accepted-manuscript detectors have DIVERGED. FIX: make them ONE shared detector (import provenance's, or
  a shared module) so they cannot drift again -- 'which stamps mark an accepted manuscript' must be a
  STATEMENT IN ONE PLACE, not duplicated. Prove: an NIH manuscript ('Author manuscript; available in PMC
  2026' + 'NIHMS2075474') -> event_ledger.derive_eligibility gives accepted_manuscript / NOT admissible,
  matching provenance.

VERIFY: build the two failing fixtures (the Nature Comms cover-sheet AM, the NIH AM) and show BOTH lanes
(census AND event_ledger) now rule them accepted_manuscript / inadmissible, agreeing with provenance.
Also confirm a REAL PMC VoR JATS is still ADMISSIBLE (no over-rejection). canary must stay green (or
greener). Do NOT touch report_ast.py (another agent owns it). Do NOT commit. Report file:line + real output.

BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. - update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.

Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).

- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). 1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""THE ALIGNMENT CENSUS — which held bytes may name a journal article, and which may not.
3	
4	Every label here is RE-DERIVED FROM THE BYTES. Three checks in this file exist because the first
5	version of it produced a confident, wrong answer, and its own audit caught it:
6	
7	 1. THE CIRCULARITY GUARD.  My first pass reported three papers "ALIGNED — all spans verified in the
8	    publishedVersion". They were not aligned. Unpaywall's `publishedVersion` URL pointed at THE SAME
9	    SPRINGER PDF ALREADY IN THE CORPUS, and I had verified the text against itself. An alignment is
10	    only an alignment if the journal bytes are a DIFFERENT DOCUMENT from the bytes we hold, so the
11	    fetched text must now clear an 8-gram distinctness test before any span is counted.
12	
13	 2. THE PUBLISHER-TYPESET TEST.  `_JOURNAL_MARK` in provenance.py matches a DOI string — but a DOI
14	    string is printed by every repository COVER SHEET, which cites the article rather than being it.
15	    The article of record is identified by furniture only a typesetter emits: the journal's name
16	    recurring as a RUNNING HEAD, and printed page FOLIOS after form-feeds, matching the bibliographic
17	    page range. That test, and not the metadata, is what proved the LSE deposit of Goos-Manning-
18	    Salomons really is the AER article (running heads "AMERICAN ECONOMIC REVIEW August 2014", folios
19	    2509-2526) -- against Unpaywall, which labels that same file `submittedVersion`.
20	
21	 3. BACKEND_FAILED IS NEVER "NO COPY".  HTTP 403 from AEA and SAGE is a fact about our request.
22	    It is recorded as ACCESS_BLOCKED and it concludes NOTHING about what exists.
23	"""
24	from __future__ import annotations
25	
26	import json
27	import re
28	from pathlib import Path
29	
30	ROOT = Path('/home/polaris/wt/flywheel')
31	
32	
33	def nz(s: str) -> str:
34	    return re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', s or '').strip()
35	
36	
37	def shingles(t: str, k: int = 8) -> set:
38	    w = nz(t).lower().split()
39	    return {' '.join(w[i:i + k]) for i in range(0, max(0, len(w) - k), 3)}
40	
41	
42	def venue_running_head_pattern(venue: str):
43	    toks = re.findall(r'[A-Za-z]{3,}', re.sub(r'&', '&', venue or ''))
44	    if not toks:
45	        return None
46	    return r'\b' + r'[\s\W]+(?:of|and|the|in|for)?[\s\W]*'.join(re.escape(t) for t in toks[:4]) + r'\b'
47	
48	
49	def folio_numbers(text: str) -> list[int]:
50	    """The printed page numbers — the integer that follows a form feed. A PDF's own pagination."""
51	    return [int(m.group(1)) for m in re.finditer(r'\x0c\s*(\d{1,5})\b', text)]
52	
53	
54	def declared_page_range(text: str, venue: str) -> tuple[int, int] | None:
55	    """The page range the document's OWN masthead prints beside the journal's name.
56	
57	    e.g. `American Economic Review 2014, 104(8): 2509-2526`. A repository cover sheet prints this too
58	    (it is citing the article) — which is exactly why the range ALONE proves nothing. It is the
59	    ANCHOR, not the evidence: what it lets us ask is whether THIS DOCUMENT'S OWN FOLIOS LAND IN IT.
60	    """
61	    p = venue_running_head_pattern(venue)
62	    if not p:
63	        return None
64	    for m in re.finditer(p, text, re.I):
65	        r = re.search(r'(\d{1,5})\s*[-–—]\s*(\d{1,5})', text[m.end():m.end() + 80])
66	        if r:
67	            lo, hi = int(r.group(1)), int(r.group(2))
68	            if 0 < hi - lo < 200:          # a page range, not a year range or a DOI fragment
69	                return lo, hi
70	    return None
71	
72	
73	def page_top_heads(text: str, venue: str, window: int = 200) -> int:
74	    """The journal's name AT THE TOP OF A PAGE — i.e. immediately after a form feed. THIS is what a
75	    running head IS, and the distinction is the whole point:
76	
77	    A repository COVER SHEET can print the journal's name (it is citing the article). A BIBLIOGRAPHY
78	    can print it thirty times (every economics paper cites the AER). NEITHER can put it at the top of
79	    three separate pages of the document's own body. Only the typesetter's page furniture does that.
80	
81	    The old test counted the venue name ANYWHERE in the text and called >=3 "running heads". On the
82	    LSE deposit of Goos-Manning-Salomons it counted 9 -- ONE masthead, ONE cover-sheet citation, and
83	    SEVEN references to other AER papers. It reached the right verdict on that file THROUGH ITS
84	    BIBLIOGRAPHY, and it would have reached the same verdict for an accepted manuscript with the same
85	    bibliography.
86	    """
87	    p = venue_running_head_pattern(venue)
88	    if not p:
89	        return 0
90	    return sum(1 for m in re.finditer(r'\x0c', text)
91	               if re.search(p, text[m.end():m.end() + window], re.I))
92	
93	
94	def typeset_profile(text: str, venue: str) -> dict:
95	    """Is this the PUBLISHER'S rendering? Only a typesetter emits page furniture."""
96	    rng = declared_page_range(text, venue)
97	    folios = folio_numbers(text)
98	    p = venue_running_head_pattern(venue)
99	    return dict(
100	        running_heads=len(re.findall(p, text, re.I)) if p else 0,   # RETAINED, but no longer decides
101	        page_top_heads=page_top_heads(text, venue),
102	        folios=len(folios),
103	        declared_range=list(rng) if rng else None,
104	        folios_in_declared_range=len([f for f in folios if rng and rng[0] <= f <= rng[1]]),
105	        wp_series_marks=len(re.findall(
106	            r'(nber working paper|working paper (no|series)|discussion paper|this draft)',
107	            text[:6000], re.I)),
108	    )
109	
110	
111	def is_publisher_typeset(tp: dict) -> bool:
112	    """PROOF, IN THE BYTES, THAT A TYPESETTER MADE THIS DOCUMENT.
113	
114	    This is the ONLY thing in this file that may overturn an inadmissible version label, so it has to
115	    be a fact a manuscript CANNOT counterfeit. Two independent proofs, either sufficient:
116	
117	      1. FOLIOS THAT LAND IN THE DECLARED PAGE RANGE. The masthead says the article runs 2509-2526 and
118	         the document's own printed page numbers ARE 2510, 2511, ... 2526. An author manuscript
119	         paginates from 1. To forge this, a manuscript would have to be typeset -- i.e. to BE the
120	         article of record. (This is the test the file's own docstring always described and the code
121	         never actually performed: it counted folios and never once checked they were in range.)
122	
123	      2. THE JOURNAL'S NAME AT THE TOP OF THREE OR MORE PAGES. A cover sheet is one page; a
124	         bibliography is not page furniture. Neither can do this.
125	    """
126	    return (tp['folios_in_declared_range'] >= 3
127	            or (tp['page_top_heads'] >= 3 and tp['folios'] >= 3))
128	
129	
130	# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
131	# THE VERSION VETO — WHICH VERSIONS ARE INADMISSIBLE IS A *STATEMENT*, NOT AN ORDERING ACCIDENT.
132	# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
133	# The V9 P0 was patched at three hops (provenance.SPAN_PRESERVING, version_align, and the `acceptedVersion`
134	# branch of the ruling ladder below) AND IT STAYED OPEN AT A FOURTH -- because the fix was a RUNG IN A
135	# LADDER. The `acceptedVersion -> INADMISSIBLE` rung sat beneath `is_publisher_typeset -> ADMISSIBLE`,
136	# and an accepted manuscript that tripped the earlier rung was ruled a JOURNAL ARTICLE and never reached
137	# its own rule. The rule was right. It was simply never asked.
138	#
139	# A rule that is correct but UNREACHABLE is not a fix, and no amount of care about the ORDER of a ladder
140	# will keep it reachable across the next edit. So the ladder no longer decides this. Inadmissibility is
141	# now a PRECONDITION, evaluated before any admitting branch may run, and re-asserted after -- Sol's
142	# principle, applied to versions: WHICH VERSIONS ARE INADMISSIBLE MUST BE A STATEMENT, NOT AN ABSENCE.
143	
144	#: A version label that can NEVER, on its own, name the journal under a journal-only policy.
145	INADMISSIBLE_VERSION_LABELS: dict[str, str] = {
146	    'acceptedVersion': ('an accepted manuscript — acceptance precedes copy-editing, proofs and the '
147	                        'editor\'s last round, and THE NUMBERS MOVE ACROSS THEM (Acemoglu & Restrepo: '
148	                        '0.37pp in the manuscript, 0.2pp in the JPE)'),
149	    'submittedVersion': ('a submitted manuscript — the working paper / preprint, before peer review '
150	                         'has touched a single number'),
151	}
152	#: ...and the only label that asserts the article of record.
153	ADMISSIBLE_VERSION_LABELS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({'publishedVersion'})
154	
155	
156	def version_statements(bid_ver: str | None, align_ver: str | None) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
157	    """EVERY authenticated version statement about these bytes, from EVERY source, in ONE list.
158	
159	    This exists so that no future source of a version label can be added without passing through the
160	    veto: a label that is not in this list cannot influence the ruling at all, and a label that IS in
161	    it is vetted by `version_veto()` before any admitting branch runs. There is no third way in.
162	    """
163	    out = []
164	    if bid_ver:
165	        out.append(('byte-identity against an authenticated backend', bid_ver))
166	    if align_ver:
167	        out.append(('a repository version label (via Unpaywall)', align_ver))
168	    return out
169	
170	
171	def version_veto(stmts: list[tuple[str, str]], typeset_ok: bool) -> tuple[str, str, str] | None:
172	    """THE PRECONDITION. Returns a refusal if ANY version statement forbids a journal attribution.
173	
174	    The ONE thing that may overturn an inadmissible label is BYTE PROOF that a typesetter made this
175	    document (`is_publisher_typeset`) -- never another label, never a metadata field, and never the
176	    order of the branches below. That escape is not a courtesy to the label: it is the reason the LSE
177	    deposit of Goos-Manning-Salomons is admissible AS THE AER ARTICLE, its folios landing inside the
178	    page range its own masthead prints, WHILE UNPAYWALL CALLS IT `submittedVersion`. The bytes outrank
179	    the label in BOTH directions -- and only the bytes ever do.
180	    """
181	    for src, label in stmts:
182	        if label in INADMISSIBLE_VERSION_LABELS:
183	            if typeset_ok:
184	                continue          # the BYTES say typesetter. A label does not outrank the bytes.
185	            kind = 'ACCEPTED_MANUSCRIPT' if label == 'acceptedVersion' else 'WORKING_PAPER'
186	            return (kind, 'INADMISSIBLE',
187	                    f'{src} says `{label}`: {INADMISSIBLE_VERSION_LABELS[label]}. Not one byte of this '
188	                    f'document carries publisher typeset furniture, so nothing overturns that label. '
189	                    f'Attributable AS AN ACCEPTED MANUSCRIPT / WORKING PAPER ONLY; a span reaches the '
190	                    f'journal only across a verified per-span SpanCorrespondence into VoR bytes')
191	    return None
192	
193	
194	def _admit(prof: dict, tp: dict, stmts: list[tuple[str, str]], bid: dict | None,
195	           oa_native, preprint) -> tuple[str, str, str]:
196	    """THE ADMITTING BRANCHES. A ruling of ADMISSIBLE can be minted HERE AND NOWHERE ELSE.
197	
198	    DEFENCE IN DEPTH: this function re-checks the veto and REFUSES TO RUN if one is live. The caller
199	    already checked it; that is the point. The P0 came back twice because a correct rule was placed
200	    where something else could answer first, so the admitting code now makes the check ITSELF, and a
201	    future refactor that calls `_admit()` directly -- or reorders `rule()` -- raises instead of
202	    admitting. You cannot get here with an accepted manuscript. There is no branch order that permits it.
203	    """
204	    typeset_ok = is_publisher_typeset(tp)
205	    veto = version_veto(stmts, typeset_ok)
206	    if veto is not None:
207	        raise AssertionError(
208	            f'_admit() was reached for a manifestation the version veto forbids ({veto[0]}). '
209	            f'THE V9 P0 HAS BEEN REOPENED BY A REORDERING. Nothing may be admitted here.')
210	
211	    if typeset_ok:
212	        if tp['folios_in_declared_range'] >= 3:
213	            return ('JOURNAL_ARTICLE', 'ADMISSIBLE',
214	                    f"publisher typeset furniture: {tp['folios_in_declared_range']} printed page folios "
215	                    f"landing inside the page range {tp['declared_range']} that this document's own "
216	                    f"masthead prints — an author manuscript paginates from 1; only the article of "
217	                    f"record is numbered with the journal's own pages")
218	        return ('JOURNAL_ARTICLE', 'ADMISSIBLE',
219	                f"publisher typeset furniture: the journal's name at the top of {tp['page_top_heads']} "
220	                f"pages, {tp['folios']} printed page folios — a cover sheet is one page and a "
221	                f"bibliography is not page furniture; only a typesetter emits these")
222	
223	    if any(lbl in ADMISSIBLE_VERSION_LABELS for _s, lbl in stmts) and bid:
224	        return ('JOURNAL_ARTICLE', 'ADMISSIBLE',
225	                f"held bytes are {bid['cover']:.3f}-identical to a location an authenticated backend "
226	                f"labels `publishedVersion` — these ARE the article of record")
227	
228	    if oa_native and tp['wp_series_marks'] == 0:
229	        return ('JOURNAL_ARTICLE', 'ADMISSIBLE',
230	                f"open-access article-of-record header block ({oa_native.group(0)[:40]!r}) and no "
231	                f"working-paper furniture anywhere in the front matter")
232	
233	    return ('UNDETERMINED_VERSION', 'INADMISSIBLE',
234	            'no publisher typeset furniture and no authenticated version statement — we cannot show '
235	            'these bytes are the article of record')
236	
237	
238	def rule(prof: dict, tp: dict, bid: dict | None, align_ver: str | None,
239	         oa_native, preprint) -> tuple[str, str, str]:
240	    """THE ONE REDUCER. Every ruling in this file is minted here — (held_kind, ruling, why).
241	
242	    Read the shape, not the branches: DISQUALIFIERS FIRST, ALL OF THEM, and only then may `_admit()`
243	    be called at all. Admissibility is what is left when nothing forbids it — it is never something a
244	    branch races to say first.
245	    """
246	    bid_ver = (bid or {}).get('version')
247	    stmts = version_statements(bid_ver, align_ver)
248	    typeset_ok = is_publisher_typeset(tp)
249	
250	    # ---- 1. THE BYTES ARE NOT USABLE EVIDENCE AT ALL. Version is moot. --------------------------
251	    if prof['identity']['verdict'] == 'CONTRADICTED':
252	        return ('WRONG_WORK', 'PURGE',
253	                'these bytes are a different work by a different author — nothing to align')
254	    if prof['artifact_kind'] == 'landing_page':
255	        return ('LANDING_PAGE', 'NO_EVIDENCE', 'a web page about the document, not the document')
256	    if prof['extractability']['verdict'] == 'CORRUPT':
257	        return ('EXTRACTION_FAILURE', 'NO_EVIDENCE', 'bytes are not readable prose')
258	
259	    # ---- 2. THE VERSION VETO. A PRECONDITION — checked before ANY admitting branch can run. -----
260	    veto = version_veto(stmts, typeset_ok)
261	    if veto is not None:
262	        return veto
263	
264	    # ---- 3. THE BYTES DISQUALIFY THEMSELVES. Also a precondition, for the same reason: a preprint
265	    #         that happens to trip an admitting branch is the identical bug with a different label.
266	    if tp['wp_series_marks'] >= 1 and not typeset_ok:
267	        return ('WORKING_PAPER', 'INADMISSIBLE',
268	                'the bytes self-declare a working-paper series and carry no publisher typeset furniture')
269	    if preprint and not oa_native and not typeset_ok:
270	        return ('PREPRINT', 'INADMISSIBLE',
271	                'the bytes say which journal they are FOR — an author preprint, not the journal’s text')
272	
273	    # ---- 4. NOTHING FORBIDS IT. Only now may anything be admitted. ------------------------------
274	    kind, ruling, why = _admit(prof, tp, stmts, bid, oa_native, preprint)
275	
276	    # ---- 5. THE POSTCONDITION. The ladder cannot be reordered into a fabrication without tripping
277	    #         this, and it does not care which branch answered or in what order.
278	    if ruling == 'ADMISSIBLE':
279	        bad = [lbl for _s, lbl in stmts if lbl in INADMISSIBLE_VERSION_LABELS]
280	        assert not bad or typeset_ok, (
281	            f'ADMISSIBLE was minted for a manifestation an authenticated backend labels {bad!r} and '
282	            f'whose bytes carry NO publisher typeset furniture. THE V9 P0 IS REOPENED.')
283	    return kind, ruling, why
284	
285	
286	BYTE_ID: dict = {}
287

291	    p = Path('/tmp/byteid.json')
292	    BYTE_ID = {k: v for k, v in (json.loads(p.read_text()) if p.exists() else {}).items() if v}

305	    out = []
306	    for i, r in enumerate(corpus):
307	        ft = r.get('fulltext') or ''
308	        if not ft.strip():
309	            continue
310	        doi = r.get('doi') or ''
311	        w = Work(id='w', title=r.get('title') or '', authors=r.get('authors') or [],
312	                 year=r.get('year'), venue=r.get('venue') or '', doi=doi)
313	        prof = profile(ft, w, r.get('abstract') or '')
314	        tp = typeset_profile(ft, r.get('venue') or '')
315	        a = align.get(i, {})
316	        n = ncards.get(doi, 0)
317	
318	        # BYTE-IDENTITY against an authenticated version statement. This route CONVICTS as readily as
319	        # it acquits: Bresnahan's held bytes are 0.999-identical to UPenn's `submittedVersion`, which
320	        # is how we know the QJE row holds the working paper.
321	        bid = BYTE_ID.get(str(i)) or BYTE_ID.get(i)

331	        preprint = re.search(r'\bfor [A-Z][a-z]+ and [A-Z][a-z]+\s*$|submitted to\b', ft[:3000], re.M)
332	
333	        # ---- THE RULING. ONE reducer, which no branch order can bypass. (See `rule()` above.) ----
334	        kind, ruling, why = rule(prof, tp, bid, (a.get('journal_bytes') or {}).get('version'),
335	                                 oa_native, preprint)

338	            idx=i, doi=doi, title=r.get('title'), venue=r.get('venue'), year=r.get('year'),
339	            authors=r.get('authors'), held_words=len(ft.split()), cards=n,
340	            wp_fetch_tag=r.get('fulltext_source'), corpus_status=r.get('content_status'),
341	            held_kind=kind, ruling=ruling, why=why, typeset=tp,

349	    bad = [r for r in out if r['ruling'] == 'INADMISSIBLE']
350	    print(f"{'idx':>3} {'cards':>5}  {'held_kind':21s} {'ruling':13s} {'venue':30s} {'doi'}")
351	    print('-' * 128)
352	    for r in sorted(out, key=lambda x: (x['ruling'] != 'INADMISSIBLE', -x['cards'])):
353	        print(f"{r['idx']:>3} {r['cards']:>5}  {r['held_kind']:21s} {r['ruling']:13s} "
354	              f"{str(r['venue'])[:30]:30s} {r['doi']}")
355	    print()

357	    print(f"  cards on INADMISSIBLE bytes : {sum(r['cards'] for r in bad):>4}  <-- must not reach the page")

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""EVENT LEDGER — every status is a PURE REDUCER over an append-only event log.
3	
4	    Sol, item 8: "NO COMPONENT MAY WRITE `complete`, `fulltext`, `no evidence`, `same work`,
5	                  or `high quality` DIRECTLY."
6	
7	THE FAILURE THIS IS THE STRUCTURAL CURE FOR
8	
9	  Every defect we have shipped had ONE shape: A LABEL THAT ASSERTED MORE THAN ITS CONTENT
10	  SUPPORTED, WITH NOTHING CHECKING. Not one announced itself; each read as a fact about the world.
11	
12	      "gate: WIRED"          -> it checked the wrong lane; fabrication shipped
13	      "span-verified"        -> verified by its FIRST 60 CHARACTERS
14	      "still paywalled"      -> we asked by DOI; the free copy is a SEPARATE WORK
15	      "FULLTEXT"             -> 535 words. A cookie banner.
16	      "no free copy exists"  -> we were HTTP 429/404. A fact about OUR REQUEST and THEIR INDEX,
17	                                disguised as a fact about the world.
18	      "working_paper"        -> written by whichever SCRIPT ran, not by what the document IS.
19	
20	  A component that CAN assert its own success will eventually assert it falsely, and nothing will
21	  catch it. So here a component may ONLY EMIT EVENTS — observations, in the vocabulary of the world:
22	  an HTTP status, a word count, a byline, a glyph ratio. The LABEL is DERIVED from those events by a
23	  reducer nobody can bypass, and the label is never stored anywhere a component could overwrite it.
24	
25	THE TWO RULES THAT MAKE IT STRUCTURAL, NOT ADVISORY
26	
27	  1. EMIT-TIME CONCLUSION GUARD. `Ledger.emit()` REJECTS any event whose payload carries a terminal
28	     conclusion — a key like `content_status`, or a value like "FULLTEXT" / "same work" /
29	     "no evidence" / "high quality". You cannot write the label into the ledger even if you try.
30	     Raw text quoted from the world (`header_text`, `span`, `document_title`) is exempt: a fetched
31	     PDF is allowed to contain the word "complete". A COMPONENT is not.
32	
33	  2. LABELS ARE NEVER STORED. They are computed on read by `derive_*` pure functions over the event
34	     list. There is no field to corrupt, no cache to go stale, and no way to "just set it".
35	
36	THE DISTINCTIONS THAT MUST NEVER COLLAPSE (Sol)
37	
38	    HTTP 429/503        -> BACKEND_FAILED.  ** NOT "no evidence exists". **
39	    HTTP 404            -> NOT_INDEXED_BY_THIS_BACKEND. A fact about THEIR INDEX. Also not absence.
40	    route_complete      -> EVERY PLANNED ADAPTER HAS A TERMINAL OUTCOME RECORD.
41	                           It must NEVER mean "an adapter was mapped."
42	    a budget stop       -> IS NOT AN EVIDENCE GAP.
43	    absence             -> only an ADEQUATE route (every adapter genuinely answered, none throttled,
44	                           none blocked, no budget stop) can support a scoped absence statement.
45	    "high_quality"      -> FORBIDDEN as a bare label. It renders as its COMPONENTS, each with
46	                           provenance: directness=high, method_quality=low, influence_percentile=0.92.
47	
48	Run it:
49	    python3 scripts/event_ledger.py            # self-test + replay of our real history
50	    python3 scripts/event_ledger.py --replay   # just the replay
51	    python3 scripts/event_ledger.py --selftest # just the invariants
52	"""

108	class ForbiddenLabel(Exception):
109	    """A component tried to write a conclusion into the ledger. That is the whole disease."""
110	
111	
112	#: Terminal conclusions. A component may not state these — the reducer derives them.
113	#:
114	#: Sol V9 §1, verbatim: "The adapter must not write FULLTEXT, THIS_WORK, VERSION_OF_RECORD, ADMISSIBLE,
115	#: or an expression edge. Those are reducer outputs derived from immutable metadata and document bytes."
116	#: FULLTEXT, ADMISSIBLE and `same work` were already here. THIS_WORK and VERSION_OF_RECORD were not —
117	#: and an adapter that may write `version_of_record` into a payload has been handed, by the back door,
118	#: precisely the authority the V9 P0 was about taking away from it. A repository saying `acceptedVersion`
119	#: is an OBSERVATION; a component writing `version_of_record` is a VERDICT.
120	RESERVED_VALUES = (
121	    'fulltext', 'full text', 'full-text',
122	    'complete', 'route_complete',
123	    'no evidence', 'no free copy', 'no free text', 'not available', 'nothing found',
124	    'same work', 'same_work', 'this work', 'this_work', 'different work', 'different_work',
125	    'version equivalent', 'version of record', 'version_of_record',
126	    'high quality', 'high_quality', 'low quality', 'low_quality',
127	    'paywalled', 'still paywalled',
128	    'supported', 'searched_none', 'conflicted',
129	    'admissible', 'inadmissible', 'eligible',
130	    'verified', 'span-verified', 'fabrication-proof',
131	)
132	
133	#: Keys that ARE labels. Naming one is an attempt to set the label directly.
134	RESERVED_KEYS = (
135	    'content_status', 'status', 'label', 'verdict', 'quality', 'tier', 'grade',
136	    'fulltext_source', 'is_fulltext', 'route_complete', 'complete', 'coverage',
137	    'coverage_status', 'eligibility', 'binding', 'admissible', 'evidence_status',
138	)
139	
140	#: Fields that are RAW TEXT QUOTED FROM THE WORLD. A PDF may contain any word at all; that is not
141	#: the component asserting it. These are exempt from the value scan (never from the key scan).
142	#: NOTE what is NOT here: `note`. A free-text `note` is the COMPONENT talking, not the world, and it
143	#: is precisely where a conclusion would be smuggled in ("note: no free copy exists"). It stays
144	#: scanned. (The guard caught exactly that in this file's own replay code, and the fix was to reword
145	#: the note into an observation — not to exempt the field.)
146	#: `locator` is here for the same reason `url` is: IT IS A URL. Publishers put our own reserved words
147	#: inside their paths (`/articles/PMC.../?report=fulltext`, `/science/article/.../fulltext`), and a
148	#: guard that refused to record THE ADDRESS THE BYTES CAME FROM — because the address contains the
149	#: string "fulltext" — would have made the locator unrecordable and left us exactly where we were:
150	#: holding an NBER working paper under a URL pointing at aeaweb.org.
151	#: `requested_venue` sits beside `requested_title` for the same reason: it is a NAME, quoted from the
152	#: bibliography. There is a journal called `Information & Management` and there could be one called
153	#: `Complete Systems`; a guard that refused to record which journal we asked about, because the
154	#: journal's name contains one of our reserved words, would be a guard against nothing.
155	VERBATIM_KEYS = frozenset({
156	    'header_text', 'identity_window', 'span', 'document_title', 'byline', 'snippet', 'raw', 'title',
157	    'reason_text', 'requested_title', 'requested_venue', 'url', 'locator', 'doi', 'venue',
158	})

161	def _scan_value(k: str, v, bad: list[str], path: str = '') -> None:
162	    """Scan ONE value, RECURSIVELY.
163	
164	    IT DID NOT RECURSE. The scan ran over `payload.items()` and tested `isinstance(v, str)` — so a
165	    conclusion one level down was invisible to it:
166	
167	        emit(..., adapter_observations={'content_status': 'FULLTEXT'})    # ACCEPTED. Silently.
168	
169	    A guard that only inspects the top level of a nested structure is a guard you step over by adding
170	    a dict, and "the checks certify a lane the fabrication no longer uses" is the sentence this whole
171	    project is trying to stop writing. Payloads stay flat by convention; this makes it true by force.
172	    """
173	    where = f'{path}{k}'
174	    if isinstance(v, str):
175	        vl = v.lower().strip()
176	        for r in RESERVED_VALUES:
177	            # match the whole value or a word-bounded occurrence — not a substring of a word
178	            if vl == r or re.search(rf'(?<![a-z0-9_]){re.escape(r)}(?![a-z0-9_])', vl):
179	                bad.append(f'{where}={v!r} states the conclusion {r!r}; emit the OBSERVATION instead '
180	                           f'(word_count / http_status / byline), and let the reducer conclude')
181	                return
182	    elif isinstance(v, dict):
183	        for k2, v2 in v.items():
184	            if str(k2).lower() in RESERVED_KEYS:
185	                bad.append(f'key {where}.{k2!r} IS a label — the reducer derives it; a component may '
186	                           f'not set it, and burying it one level down does not make it an observation')
187	                continue
188	            if str(k2).lower() in VERBATIM_KEYS:
189	                continue
190	            _scan_value(str(k2), v2, bad, path=f'{where}.')
191	    elif isinstance(v, (list, tuple)):
192	        for i, v2 in enumerate(v):
193	            _scan_value(f'[{i}]', v2, bad, path=where)
194	
195	
196	@dataclass(frozen=True)
197	class Event:
198	    """An OBSERVATION. Immutable, ordered, and never a conclusion."""
199	    seq: int
200	    unit: str                    # what this is about — a DOI, a coverage cell id, a route id
201	    kind: str
202	    actor: str                   # which component observed it (for provenance, not for authority)
203	    payload: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
204	    ts: float = field(default_factory=time.time)
205	
206	    def to_json(self) -> str:
207	        return json.dumps({'seq': self.seq, 'unit': self.unit, 'kind': self.kind,
208	                           'actor': self.actor, 'payload': self.payload, 'ts': self.ts},
209	                          sort_keys=True)
210	
211	
212	def _scan_for_conclusions(kind: str, payload: dict) -> list[str]:
213	    """Return the reasons this payload is an ASSERTION rather than an OBSERVATION."""
214	    bad: list[str] = []
215	    for k, v in payload.items():
216	        kl = str(k).lower()
217	        if kl in RESERVED_KEYS:
218	            bad.append(f'key {k!r} IS a label — the reducer derives it; a component may not set it')
219	            continue
220	        if kl in VERBATIM_KEYS:
221	            continue                       # raw text from the world. It may say anything.
222	        _scan_value(str(k), v, bad)
223	    return bad

225	
226	def _observations(events: list['Event']) -> list['Event']:
227	    """THE OBSERVATIONS ONLY. A reducer NEVER reads another reducer's recorded verdict.
228	
229	    `record_derivation()` writes audit artifacts into the same log, and the module docstring has
230	    always claimed "NO REDUCER READS THEM". That was true only BY ACCIDENT OF KIND CHOICE — nothing
231	    enforced it. It was one line from being false in the worst way: a derived record written with
232	    kind=CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED is picked up by `derive_content_profile`'s `for e in events` loop,
233	    which keeps the LAST match — so the reducer would have read ITS OWN PREVIOUS OUTPUT as if it were
234	    an observation of the bytes, and the label would have become a cache of itself. That is the cookie
235	    banner, again, from the inside.
236	
237	    So the invariant is now structural, in one place, and every derive_*() below goes through it.
238	    """
239	    return [e for e in events if 'derived_by' not in e.payload]
240	
241	
242	class LedgerCorrupt(Exception):
243	    """The persisted log does not agree with itself. It is REFUSED, never silently repaired."""
244	
245	
246	class Ledger:
247	    """Append-only, DURABLE, and RELOADABLE. You may add an observation. You may not edit, delete,
248	    or conclude.
249	
250	    IT DID NOT RELOAD. `__init__` set `self._events = []` and never read the file, so a ledger opened
251	    over an existing JSONL began with an EMPTY HISTORY — while still appending to the end of it. Every
252	    standalone script therefore reduced over the handful of events IT had emitted, not over what
253	    happened. `derive_route_status()` would find one adapter planned instead of four and call the route
254	    COMPLETE; `derive_coverage_status()` would license a scoped absence off a history it could not see.
255	    A reducer over an append-only log that does not read the log is not a reducer over anything — and
256	    it fails in the direction of CONFIDENCE, which is the only direction that ships.
257	    """
258	
259	    def __init__(self, path: Path | str | None = None, *, load: bool = True):
260	        self._events: list[Event] = []
261	        self._path = Path(path) if path else None
262	        self._seq = 0
263	        if self._path is not None:
264	            self._path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
265	            if load and self._path.exists():
266	                self._load()
267	
268	    @classmethod
269	    def load(cls, path: Path | str) -> 'Ledger':
270	        """Open the ledger AS IT STANDS ON DISK. This is the constructor a standalone script wants."""
271	        return cls(path, load=True)
272	
273	    # -- read the history back --------------------------------------------------------------------
274	    def _load(self) -> None:
275	        """A PERSISTED LEDGER IS AN UNTRUSTED INPUT, and it is exactly as untrusted as the corpus was.
276	
277	        The emit-time guard is RE-APPLIED here. Bypassing it on read would leave the door it locks
278	        standing open: `echo '{"payload":{"content_status":"FULLTEXT"}}' >> ledger.jsonl` is not a
279	        harder attack than calling emit(), it is an easier one.
280	
281	        A record written by a reducer (it carries `derived_by`) is exempt from that scan, because its
282	        whole job is to hold a derived verdict. Forging one buys nothing: NO REDUCER READS THEM. They
283	        are audit artifacts, and every derive_*() in this file re-derives from the OBSERVATIONS. That
284	        is the difference between a ledger and a cache, and caching the label is how we shipped
285	        FULLTEXT on a cookie banner.
286	        """
287	        seen: set[int] = set()
288	        events: list[Event] = []
289	        for i, line in enumerate(self._path.read_text().splitlines(), 1):
290	            line = line.strip()
291	            if not line:
292	                continue
293	            where = f'{self._path.name}:{i}'
294	            try:
295	                d = json.loads(line)
296	            except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
297	                raise LedgerCorrupt(f'{where}: not JSON — {e}')
298	            missing = {'seq', 'unit', 'kind', 'actor', 'payload', 'ts'} - set(d)
299	            if missing:
300	                raise LedgerCorrupt(f'{where}: missing field(s) {sorted(missing)}')
301	            if d['kind'] not in EventKind.ALL:
302	                raise LedgerCorrupt(f'{where}: unknown event kind {d["kind"]!r}')
303	            if not isinstance(d['payload'], dict):
304	                raise LedgerCorrupt(f'{where}: payload is not an object')
305	            if not isinstance(d['seq'], int) or d['seq'] in seen:
306	                raise LedgerCorrupt(f'{where}: seq {d["seq"]!r} is duplicated or not an integer — an '
307	                                    f'append-only log with a repeated sequence number has been edited')
308	            if 'derived_by' not in d['payload']:
309	                problems = _scan_for_conclusions(d['kind'], d['payload'])
310	                if problems:
311	                    raise LedgerCorrupt(
312	                        f'{where}: this line writes a CONCLUSION into the ledger — the emit guard '
313	                        f'refuses it, and so does the loader:\n    ' + '\n    '.join(problems))
314	            seen.add(d['seq'])
315	            events.append(Event(seq=d['seq'], unit=d['unit'], kind=d['kind'], actor=d['actor'],
316	                                payload=d['payload'], ts=d['ts']))
317	        events.sort(key=lambda e: e.seq)
318	        self._events = events
319	        self._seq = max(seen, default=0)     # ...so the next append CONTINUES the log, never collides
320	
321	    # -- write side: exactly one method, and it is guarded -------------------------------------
322	    def emit(self, unit: str, kind: str, actor: str, **payload) -> Event:
323	        if kind not in EventKind.ALL:
324	            raise ForbiddenLabel(f'unknown event kind {kind!r}')
325	        problems = _scan_for_conclusions(kind, payload)
326	        if problems:
327	            raise ForbiddenLabel(
328	                f'{actor} tried to write a CONCLUSION into the ledger:\n    ' + '\n    '.join(problems))
329	        return self._append(unit, kind, actor, payload)
330	
331	    def _append(self, unit: str, kind: str, actor: str, payload: dict) -> Event:
332	        self._seq += 1
333	        ev = Event(seq=self._seq, unit=unit, kind=kind, actor=actor, payload=dict(payload))
334	        self._events.append(ev)
335	        if self._path:
336	            # One line, one open, closed immediately: the record is with the OS before emit() returns.
337	            # An event that is in memory but not on disk is a history that disagrees with itself the
338	            # moment the process dies.
339	            with open(self._path, 'a') as fh:
340	                fh.write(ev.to_json() + '\n')
341	                fh.flush()
342	        return ev
343	
344	    # -- the reducer's audit trail --------------------------------------------------------------
345	    def record_derivation(self, unit: str, kind: str, reducer: str, inputs: list[int],
346	                          **verdict) -> Event:
347	        """A reducer may append its own verdict — FOR AUDIT ONLY. Nobody else can call this.
348	
349	        The frame check below is not decoration. `emit()` is the door components use, and it refuses
350	        conclusions. This is the door the reducer uses, and it is bolted from the inside: if the
351	        calling frame is not this module, it raises. There is no third door.
352	
353	        Downstream reducers NEVER TRUST these records — they RE-DERIVE from the observations. A
354	        recorded verdict is an audit artifact, not an authority. That is the difference between a
355	        ledger and a cache, and caching the label is how we shipped "FULLTEXT" on a cookie banner.
356	        """
357	        import inspect
358	        caller = inspect.currentframe().f_back
359	        if caller.f_globals.get('__name__') != __name__:
360	            raise ForbiddenLabel(
361	                f'{caller.f_globals.get("__name__")!r} tried to record a DERIVED label directly. '
362	                f'Only a reducer inside {__name__} may. Emit OBSERVATIONS and call derive_*().')
363	        return self._append(unit, kind, reducer,
364	                            {**verdict, 'derived_by': reducer, 'from_events': list(inputs)})
365	
366	    # -- read side ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
367	    def events(self, unit: str | None = None, kind: str | None = None) -> list[Event]:
368	        return [e for e in self._events
369	                if (unit is None or e.unit == unit) and (kind is None or e.kind == kind)]
370	
371	    def units(self) -> list[str]:
372	        return list(dict.fromkeys(e.unit for e in self._events))
373	
374	    def __len__(self) -> int:
375	        return len(self._events)

378	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
379	# OBSERVERS — pure functions from raw content to OBSERVATIONS. They conclude nothing.
380	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
381	
382	_CID = re.compile(r'\(cid:\d+\)')
383	_CHROME = re.compile(
384	    r'website uses cookies|accept\s+(?:all\s+)?cookies|by clicking the "accept" button|'
385	    r'enable javascript|sign in to (?:your account|continue)|create an account|'
386	    r'this site requires|subscribe to (?:read|view)|purchase access|add to cart|'
387	    # ...the repository LANDING PAGES that fooled us: Oxford ORA, PubMed Central, generic portals.
388	    r'skip to main content|we welcome feedback|send message|research archive|'
389	    r'privacy policy|terms of use|all rights reserved|official websites use',
390	    re.I)
391	_PUBLISHED_STAMP = re.compile(r'published version|version of record|\(refereed\)|original citation', re.I)
392	
393	#: ══ THE ACCEPTED-MANUSCRIPT STAMP — THE V9 P0'S FIFTH HOP ════════════════════════════════════════
394	#:
395	#: `_PUBLISHED_STAMP` above matches the phrase "published version". A repository cover sheet prints that
396	#: phrase IN TWO OPPOSITE SENSES, and until this stamp existed the reducer could not tell them apart:
397	#:
398	#:    LSE, on the file that IS the AER article:
399	#:        "Article (Published version) (Refereed)"        <- the library says: THIS FILE is the VoR
400	#:    White Rose, on an author's manuscript:
401	#:        "This is an author produced version of a paper published in <journal>.
402	#:         Citation for the published version: ..."       <- the library says: THE VoR IS ELSEWHERE
403	#:
404	#: The second one is a cover sheet POINTING AWAY FROM ITSELF, and `_PUBLISHED_STAMP` read it as the
405	#: document testifying that it IS the version of record. `derive_semantic_binding` returned
406	#: VERSION_PUBLISHED, `derive_eligibility` returned ADMISSIBLE, and an accepted manuscript became
407	#: journal evidence — the SAME P0 as the census ladder, in a different file, reached by a different road.
408	#:
409	#: So the sense is now decided by an explicit stamp, and this one CANNOT match LSE's "(Published
410	#: version) (Refereed)" — it matches only a statement that the file underneath is THE AUTHOR'S.
411	_ACCEPTED_STAMP = re.compile(
412	    r"(author[- ]?(produced|accepted|final)[- ]?(version|manuscript)"
413	    r"|author'?s? (accepted|final) (version|manuscript)"
414	    r"|accepted (manuscript|version)|post[- ]?print"
415	    r"|this is an? (author|accepted)|this is the (author|accepted))", re.I)
416	#: PREPRINT / WORKING-PAPER STAMPS — a DATA-DRIVEN REGISTRY, not a wall of ifs. Each row is
417	#: (repository/series key, header-regex fragment). ADDING A NEW REPOSITORY IS A ONE-LINE DATA EDIT.
418	#:
419	#: A stamp in a document's OWN header is evidence the manifestation is a WORKING-PAPER / PREPRINT
420	#: EXPRESSION — NOT the journal version of record. Peer review CHANGES NUMBERS (Acemoglu & Restrepo's
421	#: robot effect: 0.37pp in the NBER working paper, 0.2pp in the published JPE), so under a journal-only
422	#: policy a stamped manifestation is a DISCOVERY LEAD, never journal evidence (derive_semantic_binding
423	#: -> VERSION_PREPRINT -> derive_eligibility -> DISCOVERY_LEAD).
424	#:
425	#: WHY THE LIST GREW: the census caught the reducer scoring a GLO Discussion Paper and an arXiv preprint
426	#: AS JOURNAL FULLTEXT — the P0 crawling back through a gap. The old pattern knew nber|iza|arxiv but NOT
427	#: the working-paper vocabulary below, so an EconStor working paper (Gray 2013) passed as the journal
428	#: article. Whenever a new repository stamps its cover page, ADD A ROW HERE — do not touch a reducer.
429	PREPRINT_STAMP_REGISTRY: list[tuple[str, str]] = [
430	    #  registry key           header pattern (case-insensitive; matched as a fragment)
431	    ('nber',              r'nber working paper|national bureau of economic research'),
432	    ('iza',               r'iza\s+(?:dp|discussion paper)'),
433	    ('glo',               r'glo discussion paper'),
434	    ('cepr',              r'cepr discussion paper'),
435	    ('ssrn',              r'\bssrn\b'),
436	    ('repec',             r'\brepec\b'),
437	    ('mpra',              r'\bmpra\b'),
438	    ('munich_repec',      r'munich personal repec'),
439	    ('econstor',          r'\beconstor\b'),
440	    ('documento_trabajo', r'documento de trabajo'),
441	    ('cahier_recherche',  r'cahier de recherche'),
442	    ('discussion_paper',  r'discussion paper no'),
443	    ('working_paper',     r'working paper'),
444	    ('arxiv',             r'arxiv:\s*\d'),
445	    ('preprint',          r'\bpreprint\b'),
446	    ('this_version',      r'this\s+(?:draft|version):'),
447	    ('preliminary',       r'preliminary(?: and incomplete)?|do not (?:cite|quote) without'),
448	]
449	
450	#: The registry compiled to ONE alternation. Named groups let a self-test report WHICH stamp fired
451	#: (see preprint_stamp_key). observe_text scans the HEADER ONLY: every economics paper CITES NBER
452	#: working papers in its REFERENCES, and a whole-text scan reads the bibliography, not the paper —
453	#: which is exactly how a naive scan once called the actual JEP article a working paper.
454	_PREPRINT_STAMP = re.compile(
455	    '|'.join(f'(?P<{key}>{pat})' for key, pat in PREPRINT_STAMP_REGISTRY), re.I)
456	
457	
458	def preprint_stamp_key(text: str) -> str | None:
459	    """The registry key of the FIRST preprint/working-paper stamp in `text`, or None.
460	
461	    A pure OBSERVATION helper — it CONCLUDES NOTHING. derive_semantic_binding() decides what a stamp
462	    MEANS (a DIFFERENT SOURCE until version equivalence is proven); this only reports which one is
463	    present, for provenance and for the self-test that proves the registry covers each repository.
464	    """
465	    m = _PREPRINT_STAMP.search(text)
466	    return m.lastgroup if m else None

469	#: A REPOSITORY COVER SHEET IS NOT THE ARTICLE'S FRONT MATTER (Sol V9 §5).
470	#:
471	#: An institutional repository staples its own page onto the front of the PDF: "This is a repository copy
472	#: of…", the handle, the licence, and — fatally — A FULL CITATION OF THE ARTICLE, DOI AND ALL. So the
473	#: first thing a front-matter DOI extractor sees is a DOI printed by the LIBRARY, not by the publisher,
474	#: and it is printed identically whether the file underneath is the VoR, the accepted manuscript, or
475	#: (this has happened) a DIFFERENT PAPER filed under the wrong handle. A cover sheet proves what a
476	#: repository BELIEVES it deposited. The article's own front matter proves what it IS.
477	#:
478	#: So the cover sheet is SEGMENTED OFF and the article's front matter is read after it. The cover sheet
479	#: is not discarded — it is a real observation, kept under `cover_sheet_text` — it is just not allowed
480	#: to be the article's own testimony about itself.
481	#: (the pattern itself now lives in provenance._COVER_SHEET and is imported at the top of this file —
482	#:  see the note there. It is the SAME cover sheet in both lanes, so it is described in ONE place.)
483	
484	_DOI_RE = re.compile(r'\b10\.\d{4,9}/[^\s"\'<>,;)\]]+', re.I)
485	
486	#: IS THERE A BYLINE AT ALL? Sol V9 §5 lets DIFFERENT_WORK be reached by "an incompatible foreign title
487	#: PLUS A DISJOINT BYLINE" — and BOTH halves are load-bearing. A byline that is ABSENT is not a byline
488	#: that is disjoint, and the difference is the whole of this project's disease.
489	#:
490	#: This was not academic. My first cut of the rule read "no requested author appears in the front matter"
491	#: as disjointness, and the router's own demo convicted it INSIDE A MINUTE: the cookie-banner fixture —
492	#: "This website uses cookies. Sign in to continue." — came out DIFFERENT_WORK. A landing page names no
493	#: author because IT IS NOT A DOCUMENT, and reading that silence as "somebody else's paper" would have
494	#: quarantined it under a claim about authorship that nothing supported. It is a landing page. Say that.
495	#:
496	#: So a byline must be POSITIVELY OBSERVED: an explicit authorship cue, or two full names conjoined. When
497	#: this is False the reducer may not use disjointness for anything, in either direction.
498	#: An explicit authorship CUE. Case-insensitive: "By Yang-Hui He", "AUTHORS:", "Author —".
499	_BYLINE_CUE = re.compile(r'\bby\s+[A-Z]|\bauthors?\s*[:—-]', re.I)
500	#: ...or a conjoined pair of full names. CASE-SENSITIVE ON PURPOSE — names are capitalised, and folding
501	#: case here would match "the paper and the data", which is not a byline.
502	_BYLINE = re.compile(r'[A-Z][a-z]+\s+[A-Z][\w.-]+\s*(?:,|\band\b)\s*[A-Z][a-z]+\s+[A-Z][\w.-]+')
503	
504	
505	def segment_front_matter(text: str, header_chars: int = 1500) -> tuple[str, str]:
506	    """-> (cover_sheet_text, article_front_matter). Sol V9 §5: "Repository cover sheets are SEGMENTED
507	    from article front matter."
508	
509	    The boundary is taken at the first form-feed after the last cover-sheet marker (a PDF page break —
510	    the cover sheet IS a separate page, which is the one structural thing that is reliably true of it),
511	    and failing that at the end of the marker block. If no cover-sheet marker is present, the cover sheet
512	    is empty and the article's front matter is simply the header — which is the common case and must
513	    stay cheap.
514	    """
515	    head = text[:header_chars * 4]           # look further than the header: a cover sheet displaces it
516	    hits = list(_COVER_SHEET.finditer(head))
517	    if not hits:
518	        return '', text[:header_chars]
519	    end = hits[-1].end()
520	    ff = text.find('\x0c', end)
521	    boundary = ff + 1 if 0 <= ff < len(head) else end
522	    return text[:boundary], text[boundary:boundary + header_chars]
523	
524	
525	def observe_text(text: str, header_chars: int = 1500) -> dict:
526	    """Everything the ledger needs to know about a blob of retrieved text — and NOT ONE CONCLUSION.
527	
528	    Note `header_chars`: a preprint stamp is only evidence of what a document IS when it appears in
529	    the document's OWN header. Every economics paper cites NBER working papers in its REFERENCES —
530	    scanning the whole text for "NBER Working Paper" identifies the bibliography, not the paper.
531	    (That naive scan called the actual Journal of Economic Perspectives article a working paper.)
532	    """
533	    words = text.split()
534	    header = text[:header_chars]
535	    # THE ARTICLE'S OWN FRONT MATTER, with any repository cover sheet segmented off it. The DOIs found
536	    # HERE are the document's own claim about which article it is — as distinct from the DOI a library
537	    # printed on a cover page, which is the library's claim about what it filed.
538	    cover, article_front = segment_front_matter(text, header_chars)
539	    front_dois = sorted({d.rstrip('.').lower() for d in _DOI_RE.findall(article_front)})
540	    cid_tokens = sum(1 for t in words if 'cid:' in t)
541	    stripped = _CID.sub('', text)
542	    real_words = len(re.findall(r'[A-Za-z]{3,}', stripped))
543	    # How much REAL text is in the header? A title page of pure (cid:NN) glyph codes decodes to
544	    # nothing — and a header we cannot READ is a header we cannot CHECK AN IDENTITY AGAINST. It is
545	    # not a stranger's paper. It is an unreadable one, and those are different facts.
546	    header_real = len(re.findall(r'[A-Za-z]{3,}', _CID.sub('', header)))
547	    return {
548	        'word_count': len(words),
549	        'char_count': len(text),
550	        'readable_word_count': real_words,
551	        'glyph_garbage_ratio': round(cid_tokens / max(1, len(words)), 4),
552	        'chrome_markers': len(_CHROME.findall(text)),
553	        # ---- VERSION FURNITURE, READ IN THE RIGHT VOICE ----------------------------------------
554	        # A stamp means something different depending on WHO PRINTED IT. The article's own front
555	        # matter is the DOCUMENT'S TESTIMONY ABOUT ITSELF; the cover sheet is THE LIBRARY'S OPINION OF
556	        # WHAT IT FILED. These were previously read from the same undifferentiated `header` slice, so a
557	        # library's "citation for the published version" was scored as the article calling itself the
558	        # version of record. They are separated now, and `derive_semantic_binding` weighs them by voice.
559	        'has_cover_sheet': bool(cover),
560	        'published_stamp_in_header': bool(_PUBLISHED_STAMP.search(article_front)),
561	        'published_stamp_in_cover': bool(_PUBLISHED_STAMP.search(cover)),
562	        'accepted_stamp_in_header': bool(_ACCEPTED_STAMP.search(article_front)),
563	        'accepted_stamp_in_cover': bool(_ACCEPTED_STAMP.search(cover)),
564	        'preprint_stamp_in_header': bool(_PREPRINT_STAMP.search(article_front)),
565	        'preprint_stamp_in_cover': bool(_PREPRINT_STAMP.search(cover)),
566	        'preprint_stamp_anywhere': bool(_PREPRINT_STAMP.search(text)),
567	        'header_real_words': header_real,
568	        # the window identity is checked in. BOUNDED ON PURPOSE: searching the WHOLE document for an
569	        # author name matches the REFERENCES — every requested author is "present" somewhere in every
570	        # paper. (A mathematician named Parry is cited in the maths paper we misfiled under Parry
571	        # et al. A whole-text search "confirmed" the wrong paper.)
572	        'identity_window': header,
573	        'header_text': header[:400],
574	        # ---- SOL V9 §5: IDENTITY FROM THE FETCHED CONTENT ITSELF -------------------------------
575	        # The DOI the ARTICLE prints on itself (JATS article-DOI, PDF front matter, HTML header) — with
576	        # any repository COVER SHEET segmented off, because a cover sheet's DOI is the library's opinion
577	        # of what it deposited, not the document's testimony about what it is.
578	        'front_matter_dois': front_dois,
579	        'cover_sheet_chars': len(cover),
580	        'cover_sheet_text': cover[:400],
581	        'article_front_matter': article_front[:400],
582	        # A BYLINE IS PRESENT — or it is not. `False` means the reducer may not reason about
583	        # disjointness AT ALL: an absent byline is not a disjoint one. (A cookie banner names no author
584	        # because it is not a document, not because a stranger wrote it.)
585	        'byline_present': bool(_BYLINE_CUE.search(article_front) or _BYLINE.search(article_front)),
586	    }

589	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
590	# REDUCERS — the ONLY place a label may come into existence.
591	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
592	
593	# ---- 1. backend outcome ----------------------------------------------------------------------
594	
595	RESPONDED            = 'RESPONDED'                    # the backend answered with content
596	NOT_INDEXED          = 'NOT_INDEXED_BY_THIS_BACKEND'  # 404 — a fact about THEIR INDEX, not the world
597	BACKEND_FAILED       = 'BACKEND_FAILED'               # 429/503/timeout — a fact about OUR REQUEST
598	ACCESS_BLOCKED       = 'ACCESS_BLOCKED'               # 401/403/paywall — a fact about ENTITLEMENT
599	NO_OUTCOME           = 'NO_OUTCOME'                   # attempted, never came back. A HANG, not a gap.
600	
601	
602	#: The four kinds that constitute A REQUEST. CANDIDATE_IDENTIFIED and MANIFESTATION_FETCHED also carry
603	#: an `adapter` (for provenance — WHICH backend found this), but they are not request outcomes and must
604	#: not be read as one.
605	_REQUEST_KINDS = (EventKind.BACKEND_ATTEMPTED, EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED,
606	                  EventKind.THROTTLED, EventKind.BLOCKED)
607	
608	#: Worst-first. An adapter that hung on one request and answered another has NOT answered: we asked it
609	#: something and never found out. Between RESPONDED and NOT_INDEXED, RESPONDED wins — a backend that
610	#: answered "not in my index" for one query and returned a paper for another IS WORKING.
611	_OUTCOME_PRECEDENCE = (NO_OUTCOME, BACKEND_FAILED, ACCESS_BLOCKED, RESPONDED, NOT_INDEXED)
612	
613	
614	def _outcome_of_request(evs: list[Event]) -> str:
615	    """The outcome of ONE logical request, read from its FINAL terminal event.
616	
617	    A retry loop emits BACKEND_ATTEMPTED + one terminal event PER ATTEMPT, all sharing a `request_id`.
618	    So a request reads:  ATTEMPTED, THROTTLED, ATTEMPTED, THROTTLED, ATTEMPTED, RESPONSE_RECEIVED(200)
619	
620	    The 429s ARE NOT DELETED — they are on disk forever, and `Ledger` has no operation that could
621	    remove them. But a throttle the backoff RESOLVED and a throttle we GAVE UP ON are different facts
622	    about the world, and only the second one means we never got the answer. Reading "any 429 anywhere"
623	    as BACKEND_FAILED would mark every route degraded the moment a single retry succeeded, and a route
624	    that is always degraded can never license a scoped absence — which sounds conservative and is
625	    actually just as blind as always calling it complete.
626	
627	        exhausted retries -> final terminal event is THROTTLED         -> BACKEND_FAILED. FOREVER.
628	        429 then 200      -> final terminal event is RESPONSE_RECEIVED -> RESPONDED.
629	    """
630	    terminal = [e for e in evs if e.kind in (EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED, EventKind.THROTTLED,
631	                                             EventKind.BLOCKED)]
632	    if not terminal:
633	        return NO_OUTCOME               # attempted and never came back. A HANG, not a gap.
634	    e = terminal[-1]
635	    if e.kind == EventKind.THROTTLED:
636	        return BACKEND_FAILED
637	    if e.kind == EventKind.BLOCKED:
638	        return ACCESS_BLOCKED
639	    code = e.payload.get('http_status')
640	    if code in (429, 503):
641	        return BACKEND_FAILED           # even if someone logged it as a "response"
642	    if code in (401, 403):
643	        return ACCESS_BLOCKED
644	    if code == 404:
645	        return NOT_INDEXED
646	    if e.payload.get('transport_error'):
647	        return BACKEND_FAILED           # timeout / DNS / reset / bad JSON / 5xx
648	    return RESPONDED
649	
650	
651	# ---- 1b. ROUTE LINEAGE — WHOSE DOCUMENT IS THIS? (Sol V9 §1) ---------------------------------
652	#
653	# THE BUG THIS EXISTS TO CLOSE: a route could be credited with a document some OTHER route fetched.
654	#
655	# The chain is:  resolver request -> candidate -> content request -> redirects -> manifestation
656	# and `candidate_id` is the thread that runs through all of it. A MANIFESTATION_FETCHED event carries the
657	# CONTENT HOST in `adapter` (`content:arxiv.org`) — never the resolver that proposed the URL — so
658	# "which adapter found this document?" is NOT answerable from the manifestation alone. It is answerable
659	# only by walking back to the CANDIDATE_IDENTIFIED event that minted the candidate_id, whose `adapter`
660	# IS the resolver.
661	#
662	# Reducing over "all manifestations of the unit", as the discovery reducer did, answers a different
663	# question — "did ANYBODY get a document for this work?" — and reports it once per route. Every route
664	# then inherits every other route's success, unique incremental yield is identically equal to gross
665	# yield, and the ladder Sol asks for in §9 measures nothing at all.
666	
667	
668	def candidates_of_adapter(events: list[Event], adapter: str) -> set[str]:
669	    """The candidate_ids THIS ADAPTER PROPOSED. The lineage root — an adapter owns what it discovered."""
670	    return {str(e.payload['candidate_id']) for e in _observations(events)
671	            if e.kind == EventKind.CANDIDATE_IDENTIFIED
672	            and e.payload.get('adapter') == adapter
673	            and e.payload.get('candidate_id')}
674	
675	
676	def manifestations_of_adapter(events: list[Event], adapter: str) -> list[Event]:
677	    """The MANIFESTATION_FETCHED events that descend from a candidate `adapter` proposed.
678	
679	    Also includes manifestations whose OWN adapter is `adapter` — a content host IS a route (if nber.org
680	    403s us we were blocked BY NBER), and when a fetcher pulls a URL with no candidate lineage the
681	    content host is the only route there is.
682	
683	    A manifestation with NO candidate_id and a different adapter belongs to NOBODY, and is therefore
684	    credited to nobody. That is the safe default and the honest one: we do not know which route earned
685	    it, and inventing an answer is the failure this reducer exists to end.
686	    """
687	    mine = candidates_of_adapter(events, adapter)
688	    out = []
689	    for e in _observations(events):
690	        if e.kind != EventKind.MANIFESTATION_FETCHED:
691	            continue
692	        cid = str(e.payload.get('candidate_id') or '')
693	        if (cid and cid in mine) or e.payload.get('adapter') == adapter:
694	            out.append(e)
695	    return out
696	
697	
698	def events_of_adapter_lineage(events: list[Event], adapter: str) -> list[Event]:
699	    """This unit's events with EVERY OTHER ROUTE'S DOCUMENTS REMOVED — and their profiles with them.
700	
701	    This is what lets the existing content reducers (`derive_content_profile`, `derive_semantic_binding`)
702	    answer "what did THIS ROUTE get?" without any of them learning a second vocabulary. They still reduce
703	    over an event list; the list is just no longer somebody else's.
704	
705	    MANIFESTATION_FETCHED and its CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED are emitted ADJACENTLY and are paired by
706	    adjacency downstream (`_holdings`), so a foreign manifestation must take its profile out with it.
707	    Dropping the manifestation alone would leave an orphan profile that pairs with the NEXT
708	    manifestation — one route's bytes wearing another route's word count, which is the same class of bug
709	    one layer down.
710	    """
711	    keep = {id(e) for e in manifestations_of_adapter(events, adapter)}
712	    out: list[Event] = []
713	    drop_profile = False
714	    for e in events:
715	        if e.kind == EventKind.MANIFESTATION_FETCHED and 'derived_by' not in e.payload:
716	            drop_profile = id(e) not in keep
717	            if not drop_profile:
718	                out.append(e)
719	            continue
720	        if e.kind == EventKind.CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED and drop_profile:
721	            drop_profile = False
722	            continue
723	        drop_profile = False
724	        out.append(e)
725	    return out
726	
727	
728	def derive_backend_outcome(events: list[Event], adapter: str) -> str:
729	    """What actually happened to one adapter. `None` is not an answer — it is four different worlds.
730	
731	    An adapter may be asked more than once (four candidate PDFs from one content host; a DOI lookup and
732	    a title search). Each logical request is scored on its own; the adapter reports its WORST.
733	    """
734	    mine = [e for e in _observations(events)
735	            if e.payload.get('adapter') == adapter and e.kind in _REQUEST_KINDS]
736	    if not mine:
737	        return NO_OUTCOME
738	
739	    # Events carrying no `request_id` are a HISTORY WRITTEN BEFORE RETRIES EXISTED (our replay, the
740	    # hand-written invariants, any older log). They are one bucket, scored by the original rule: ANY
741	    # throttle anywhere condemns the adapter. Back-compat is not politeness here — a reducer that
742	    # changed its answer about a log it had already reduced would make the whole record unreadable.
743	    buckets: dict[str, list[Event]] = {}
744	    for e in mine:
745	        buckets.setdefault(str(e.payload.get('request_id') or ''), []).append(e)
746	
747	    outcomes: list[str] = []
748	    for rid, evs in buckets.items():
749	        if rid == '':
750	            if any(e.kind == EventKind.THROTTLED for e in evs):
751	                outcomes.append(BACKEND_FAILED)
752	            elif any(e.kind == EventKind.BLOCKED for e in evs):
753	                outcomes.append(ACCESS_BLOCKED)
754	            else:
755	                first = [e for e in evs if e.kind == EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED][:1]
756	                outcomes.append(_outcome_of_request(first) if first else NO_OUTCOME)
757	        else:

760	    for o in _OUTCOME_PRECEDENCE:

763	    return NO_OUTCOME
764	
765	
766	# ---- 2. route status -------------------------------------------------------------------------
767	
768	@dataclass(frozen=True)
769	class RouteStatus:
770	    state: str                     # UNROUTED | INCOMPLETE | COMPLETE_DEGRADED | COMPLETE
771	    planned: tuple[str, ...]
772	    outcomes: dict[str, str]
773	    missing: tuple[str, ...]       # planned adapters with NO terminal outcome
774	    failed: tuple[str, ...]        # adapters that were throttled/blocked/errored
775	    budget_stopped: bool
776	
777	    @property
778	    def supports_absence(self) -> bool:
779	        """THE question. Only an ADEQUATE route licenses "we looked, and it is not there."
780	
781	        Every planned adapter must have genuinely ANSWERED. One 429, one hang, or a budget stop and
782	        the honest label is SEARCH_FAILED — we do not know, and we may not say we do.
783	        """
784	        return (self.state == 'COMPLETE'
785	                and not self.missing and not self.failed and not self.budget_stopped
786	                and bool(self.planned))

789	def derive_route_status(events: list[Event]) -> RouteStatus:
790	    """route_complete means EVERY PLANNED ADAPTER HAS A TERMINAL OUTCOME RECORD.
791	
792	    It NEVER means "an adapter was mapped".
793	    """
794	    events = _observations(events)
795	    planned: list[str] = []
796	    for e in events:
797	        if e.kind == EventKind.ROUTE_PLANNED:
798	            planned += [a for a in e.payload.get('adapters', []) if a not in planned]
799	    if not planned:
800	        return RouteStatus('UNROUTED', (), {}, (), (), False)
801	
802	    budget = any(e.kind == EventKind.BUDGET_STOPPED for e in events)
803	    outcomes = {a: derive_backend_outcome(events, a) for a in planned}
804	    missing = tuple(a for a, o in outcomes.items() if o == NO_OUTCOME)
805	
806	    # ── A BLOCKED RETRIEVAL IS A FAILED ROUTE. ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
807	    # The SEARCH adapters are the ones in ROUTE_PLANNED. But finding the document's address is not
808	    # holding the document: after a search answers, we go to the CONTENT HOST for the bytes, and that
809	    # host is a backend too — it can throttle us, and it can shut the door in our face.
810	    #
811	    # This was live, and it was the whole disease wearing a new coat. Autor (2015), JEP:
812	    #
813	    #     crossref                RESPONDED        (the metadata)
814	    #     unpaywall               RESPONDED        (...and here is where the PDF lives)
815	    #     content:www.aeaweb.org  ACCESS_BLOCKED   HTTP 403
816	    #
817	    # and because the content host was not a PLANNED adapter, the route read COMPLETE, `supports_
818	    # absence` read TRUE, and coverage reduced to SEARCHED_NONE — "a SCOPED ABSENCE, and the only
819	    # state that licenses one". A 403 FROM THE PUBLISHER had become a statement that the literature
820	    # is silent. That is a fact about ENTITLEMENT, printed as a fact about the world: the identical
821	    # error to reading a 429 as "no free copy exists", committed one layer down, by the cure.
822	    #
823	    # So EVERY adapter that failed counts against the route — planned or not. We do not get to say we
824	    # looked and found nothing when we were shown the door.
825	    all_adapters = {e.payload.get('adapter') for e in events
826	                    if e.kind in _REQUEST_KINDS and e.payload.get('adapter')}
827	    for a in sorted(all_adapters - set(planned)):
828	        o = derive_backend_outcome(events, a)
829	        if o in (BACKEND_FAILED, ACCESS_BLOCKED):
830	            outcomes[a] = o
831	    failed = tuple(a for a, o in outcomes.items() if o in (BACKEND_FAILED, ACCESS_BLOCKED))
832	
833	    if missing:

835	    elif failed or budget:
836	        state = 'COMPLETE_DEGRADED'     # every adapter has a record — but the search DID NOT WORK
837	    else:
838	        state = 'COMPLETE'

842	# ---- 3. content profile — DRIVEN BY THE ONE REGISTRY, NOT BY A NUMBER ------------------------

846	# It was written in the fix for the bug it is. `provenance.py`'s own header says, in Sol's words: "A
847	# universal word threshold cannot define 'full text': a short judicial opinion, statute section,
848	# trial-registry record, and journal article have different completeness profiles" — and then this
849	# module, the CURE, reinstated one universal number two files away, where nothing compared them. It
850	# did not merely contradict the principle; it contradicted `provenance.profile()`, whose scholarly
851	# floor is 1,200. TWO REDUCERS THAT DISAGREE ARE NOT A SHARED RULE. Which document a card could cite
852	# depended on which module happened to look at it last.

854	# So completeness is not decided here at all. It is READ FROM THE REGISTRY, by the ONE reducer:

864	C_CITATION     = 'CITATION_ONLY'
865	C_NOT_DOC      = 'NOT_A_DOCUMENT'        # a cookie banner, a login wall, a paywall interstitial
866	C_UNREADABLE   = 'UNREADABLE_ENCODING'   # a PDF whose glyphs never decoded
867	
868	GLYPH_GARBAGE_MAX = 0.10   # > this share of (cid:NN) tokens => the font never decoded
869	CHROME_PER_1K_MAX = 5.0    # web furniture DENSITY. Density, not length — see below.
870	
871	#: THE LEGACY THREE-WAY VOCABULARY. `cellcog_composer` and `evidence_miner` both select their usable
872	#: corpus with `content_status != 'CITATION_ONLY'`, so the coarse label they contract on is a
873	#: PROJECTION of the five-way derived class — computed in ONE place, here, and never by a word count.
874	#:

876	#: CITATION_ONLY — that is, TO UNUSABLE. It is tempting to send them to ABSTRACT_ONLY (they have
877	#: hundreds of words, after all, and a word-count rule sends them there automatically — which is what
878	#: `corpus_truth.FULLTEXT_MIN=2500` did). That would make a COOKIE BANNER MINABLE. The 535-word aeaweb
879	#: banner and the 548-word ORA landing page would both have passed `!= 'CITATION_ONLY'` and been mined

Both P0 hops are CLOSED. All three lanes now agree, no over-rejection, canary unchanged from baseline. Nothing committed.

## What was wrong and what I changed

**HOP 7 — the counterfeit typeset** (`scripts/alignment_census.py`)
`typeset_profile` derived every admitting signal (`declared_page_range`, `folio_numbers`, `page_top_heads`) from the *whole* text, cover sheet included. A repository cover sheet CITES the article it wraps, so a Nature Communications accepted manuscript whose cover sheet prints "Nature Communications 14(1): 1-12" and whose body paginates from 1 had folios 1–10 "land in [1,12]" → `is_publisher_typeset` True → ruled the JOURNAL ARTICLE.
Fix: import `provenance.segment_cover_sheet` (`alignment_census.py:39`) and segment the cover sheet off FIRST, deriving folios / declared range / running heads from the article body only (`alignment_census.py:123`). `wp_series_marks` still reads the whole text (a cover sheet MAY convict, may never acquit).

**HOP 5 — detector drift** (`scripts/event_ledger.py`)
`event_ledger._ACCEPTED_STAMP` was a second, hand-maintained copy of `provenance._AM_MARK`. Hop 6 widened `_AM_MARK` with the NIH tells (`author manuscript; available in pmc`, `\bnihms\s*\d{3,}`); the copy here never learned them, so an NIH author manuscript was scored `VERSION_PUBLISHED`/ADMISSIBLE here while provenance ruled it `accepted_manuscript`.
Fix: deleted the local regex and bound the name to the imported detector — `from provenance import ... _AM_MARK as _ACCEPTED_STAMP` (`event_ledger.py:81`). One statement, one place; the two lanes cannot diverge again.

| **A** Nature Comms AM (cover cites 14(1):1-12, body from 1) [HOP 7] | `accepted_manuscript` | `ACCEPTED_MANUSCRIPT` / **INADMISSIBLE** | `VERSION_OF_ACCEPTED` / **DISCOVERY_LEAD** |
